Certainly, to make this method fully rigorous, it remains to show the theoretic convergence of the single-valued fixed-point iteration, which is a nontrivial problem in itself and will be left for future research.
title: Convergence of single-valued fixed-point iteration for Gibbs-form equilibrium operators
background_latex:
Consider a discrete-time infinite-horizon Markov decision process (MDP) with finite state space \(S=\{1,\dots,d\}\) and compact action space \(U\subset \mathbb{R}^\ell\) with \(0<\mathrm{Leb}(U)<\infty\). A relaxed (randomized) feedback control is a map \(\pi:S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\), where \(\mathcal{P}(U)\) denotes probability measures on \(U\). For \(\lambda>0\), an entropy-regularized objective is used in which the stage payoff includes a Shannon differential entropy term. Under entropy regularization, the equilibrium (in the sense of intra-personal subgame-perfect equilibrium for time-inconsistent discounting) can be characterized as a fixed point of a single-valued operator of Gibbs (softmax) form.

More concretely, for each state \(i\in S\) and vector \(y\in\mathbb{R}^d\), define a probability density \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)\in \mathcal{D}(U)\) by
\[\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)(u):=\frac{\exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,u)+p_i^u\cdot y)\right)}{\int_U \exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,v)+p_i^v\cdot y)\right)\,dv},\qquad u\in U,\]
where \(f(0,i,u)\) is the one-step reward (at time-difference \(0\)) and \(p_i^u\in\mathbb{R}^d\) is the transition-probability row vector from state \(i\) under action \(u\). Given \(y\), the corresponding relaxed control is \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot):S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\). Let \(J_\lambda^\pi\) be the entropy-regularized value functional and let \(V_\lambda^\pi\in\mathbb{R}^d\) be the associated continuation-value vector (expected future discounted reward plus discounted entropy starting one step ahead). Define the induced single-valued self-map \(\Psi_\lambda:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^d\) by
\[\Psi_\lambda(y):=V_\lambda^{\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)}.\]
Fixed points \(y^\star\) of \(\Psi_\lambda\) correspond to entropy-regularized relaxed equilibria \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\).

A natural numerical approach is fixed-point iteration on \(\Psi_\lambda\) (equivalently on the policy operator \(\Phi_\lambda(\pi):=\Gamma_\lambda(V_\lambda^\pi,\cdot)\)), i.e., start from some \(y_0\in\mathbb{R}^d\) (or \(\pi_0\)) and generate \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) (or \(\pi_{n+1}=\Phi_\lambda(\pi_n)\)).
problem_definition_latex:
Give conditions under which the single-valued fixed-point iteration
\[y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\qquad (n\ge 0)
\]
converges (e.g., from arbitrary initialization, or from some specified set of initial points) to a fixed point \(y^\star\in\mathbb{R}^d\) satisfying \(\Psi_\lambda(y^\star)=y^\star\), equivalently producing a regular relaxed equilibrium \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\) for the entropy-regularized time-inconsistent MDP.
latex:
Consider a discrete-time infinite-horizon Markov decision process (MDP) with finite state space \(S=\{1,\dots,d\}\) and compact action space \(U\subset \mathbb{R}^\ell\) with \(0<\mathrm{Leb}(U)<\infty\). A relaxed (randomized) feedback control is a map \(\pi:S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\), where \(\mathcal{P}(U)\) denotes probability measures on \(U\). For \(\lambda>0\), an entropy-regularized objective is used in which the stage payoff includes a Shannon differential entropy term. Under entropy regularization, the equilibrium (in the sense of intra-personal subgame-perfect equilibrium for time-inconsistent discounting) can be characterized as a fixed point of a single-valued operator of Gibbs (softmax) form.

More concretely, for each state \(i\in S\) and vector \(y\in\mathbb{R}^d\), define a probability density \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)\in \mathcal{D}(U)\) by
\[\Gamma_\lambda(y,i)(u):=\frac{\exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,u)+p_i^u\cdot y)\right)}{\int_U \exp\left(\frac{1}{\lambda}(f(0,i,v)+p_i^v\cdot y)\right)\,dv},\qquad u\in U,\]
where \(f(0,i,u)\) is the one-step reward (at time-difference \(0\)) and \(p_i^u\in\mathbb{R}^d\) is the transition-probability row vector from state \(i\) under action \(u\). Given \(y\), the corresponding relaxed control is \(\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot):S\to \mathcal{P}(U)\). Let \(J_\lambda^\pi\) be the entropy-regularized value functional and let \(V_\lambda^\pi\in\mathbb{R}^d\) be the associated continuation-value vector (expected future discounted reward plus discounted entropy starting one step ahead). Define the induced single-valued self-map \(\Psi_\lambda:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^d\) by
\[\Psi_\lambda(y):=V_\lambda^{\Gamma_\lambda(y,\cdot)}.\]
Fixed points \(y^\star\) of \(\Psi_\lambda\) correspond to entropy-regularized relaxed equilibria \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\).

A natural numerical approach is fixed-point iteration on \(\Psi_\lambda\) (equivalently on the policy operator \(\Phi_\lambda(\pi):=\Gamma_\lambda(V_\lambda^\pi,\cdot)\)), i.e., start from some \(y_0\in\mathbb{R}^d\) (or \(\pi_0\)) and generate \(y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\) (or \(\pi_{n+1}=\Phi_\lambda(\pi_n)\)).

Give conditions under which the single-valued fixed-point iteration
\[y_{n+1}=\Psi_\lambda(y_n)\qquad (n\ge 0)
\]
converges (e.g., from arbitrary initialization, or from some specified set of initial points) to a fixed point \(y^\star\in\mathbb{R}^d\) satisfying \(\Psi_\lambda(y^\star)=y^\star\), equivalently producing a regular relaxed equilibrium \(\pi^\star=\Gamma_\lambda(y^\star,\cdot)\) for the entropy-regularized time-inconsistent MDP.
open_problem_quote: Certainly, to make this method fully rigorous, it remains to show the theoretic convergence of the single-valued fixed-point iteration, which is a nontrivial problem in itself and will be left for future research.
counterexample_suitability_rationale: One can computationally test convergence or non-convergence of the iteration on many randomly generated finite-state instances by explicitly evaluating \(\Psi_\lambda\) and iterating, but the space of instances is large and failure modes may be sensitive to parameter choices and initialization.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: Candidate updates (damping, averaging, alternative operators, or sufficient-condition discovery) can be represented as code and scored by an automatic fitness signal such as empirical convergence rate or basin of attraction over a distribution of MDP instances, making evolutionary search plausible for discovering robustly convergent schemes or sharp conjectures.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
